title: First Slack message → tenant provisioned → container started → message delivered

title: Repeated messages from same user → same tenant reused

title: Stopped tenant → wakes on next message → queued messages replayed
evidence: All 7 TC-003 assertions passed in 4317ms (109/109 total e2e tests pass): - ✓ Tenant ACTIVE after initial provisioning - ✓ POST /stop → status = STOPPED, dc.stop() called - ✓ Relay returns 200 immediately for message sent to stopped tenant - ✓ Relay calls `/v1/tenants/:id/start` (tracked via fetch interceptor) - ✓ Tenant wakes to ACTIVE (dc.start() called, not dc.run()) - ✓ Ev_TC003_MSG1 (sent while STOPPED) → DELIVERED after wake-up - ✓ Ev_TC003_MSG2 (sent after wake) → DELIVERED; MSG1.created_at < MSG2.created_at (FIFO)

title: Concurrent messages to stopped tenant → single start (startup lock)

title: Startup lock → no duplicate containers on concurrent messages
evidence: All 5 TC-013 tests passed in 24ms (330/330 total tests, 49 test files): - ✓ TC-013: step 1 — acquireStartupLock(requestId1) returns {acquired: true} - ✓ TC-013: step 2 — concurrent acquireStartupLock(requestId2) returns {acquired: false} - ✓ TC-013: step 3 — after releaseStartupLock(requestId1), acquireStartupLock(requestId3) returns {acquired: true} - ✓ TC-013: step 4 — stale lock (expires_at 1ms ago) is overridden, {acquired: true} - ✓ TC-013: full sequence — mutual exclusion and stale takeover At most one lock holder enforced; stale locks expire and can be taken over.
